title: Algorithmic Game Theory: Key Concepts & Connections
description: A flowchart illustrating the core components of Algorithmic Game Theory and their interrelationships, focusing on how computational and game-theoretic concepts merge.
elements: Game Models (e.g., Normal Form, Extensive Form)
Solution Concepts (e.g., Nash Equilibrium, Pareto Efficiency)
relationships: Game Models are analyzed using Solution Concepts
Algorithm Design is constrained by Computational Complexity
Mechanism Design uses Algorithm Design to create games with desirable outcomes
Price of Anarchy measures the efficiency gap caused by strategic behavior
Solution Concepts are computed or approximated by algorithms
